rulings.Case Title: LINDA S CATRON VS. NATIONSTAR MORTGAGE LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Vacate Order To Take Matter Off Calendar Excusabl; Court Error; Under Ccc 473 Motion Was Served With Summons Plaintiff Notice And Motion To Exetend Time
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 26, 2026. Line 1. PLAINTIFF LINDA CATRON AN INDIVIDUAL Motion To Vacate Order To Take Matter Off Calendar is OFF CALENDAR. No valid proof of service on file; no remittitur on file (notice of appeal filed on January 13, 2026); no notice on file re: lifting bankruptcy stay (notice of stay filed on September 25, 2025). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DELAWARE LAKEWOOD APARTMENTS, LLC VS. CRAIG GOMEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 26, 2026. Line 8. PLAINTIFF DELAWARE LAKEWOOD APARTMENTS, LLC MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS is DENIED. As reflected in the Court's previous ruling overruling Defendant's demurrer, the Court agreed that the demurrer was not meritorious. However, Plaintiff's argument that Defense counsel should have done more or better legal research into the applicable unlawful detainer statutes and case law before filing the demurrer does not make the demurrer a frivolous filing. Sanctions are therefore denied. Defense counsel is nonetheless admonished to ensure that all of her future filings adhere to the standards articulated in CCP 128.7(b). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
